purpose: Assess how AI enables or constrains business growth by testing the alignment between capability, business readiness, adoption, delivered value, and commercial impact.

examples: In recent wins, where did AI clearly strengthen the reason the buyer chose the offer?
Which opportunities were won because AI created a credible advantage?
Where did AI materially improve perceived value?
In recent losses, where did buyers doubt the AI story enough to weaken confidence?
Which opportunities failed because AI claims were not believable?
Where did the AI proposition fail to earn trust?
Across live deals, where is AI being positioned beyond what can be delivered?
Which pursuits carry risk because the AI story is ahead of capability?
Where is promise stretching beyond operational reality?
In delivered work, where has AI failed to perform as sold?
Which use cases exposed a gap between proposed and actual capability?
Where has AI under-delivered against expectation?
Where is AI creating measurable business value rather than noise?
Which use cases are producing clear operational or financial impact?
Where is AI improving outcomes in a way that matters?
Which AI use cases are consistently used in day-to-day operations?
Where has AI become embedded in how work is done?
Which use cases show sustained usage rather than initial interest?
Where are users resisting AI strongly enough to limit its impact?
Which use cases fail because teams do not adopt them?
Where is behaviour preventing AI from being effective?
Where has AI been deployed but is not used enough to create value?
Which use cases exist but are ignored in real workflows?
Where is capability present but adoption is weak?
Which parts of the business are structurally ready to adopt AI at scale?
Where does the operating model already support AI integration?
Which teams can absorb AI without major disruption?
Where is AI failing because the business is not ready to support it?
Which use cases break due to missing processes, skills, or governance?
Where does lack of readiness undermine AI outcomes?
Where does AI align cleanly with how work is structured today?
Which use cases fit naturally into existing workflows?
Where is AI succeeding because the operating model supports it?
Where is organisational resistance limiting AI adoption or impact?
Which teams reject or bypass AI capabilities?
Where does culture block AI from being effective?
Where has AI made it easier to win or scale target opportunities?
Which growth ambitions are now achievable because of AI?
Where does AI unlock access to higher-value work?
Which opportunities are harder to win because AI capability is not strong enough?
Where is lack of AI limiting growth ambition?
Which target opportunities remain out of reach?
Which AI use cases are viable enough to scale commercially?
Where are use cases attractive in theory but weak in practice?
Which use cases should be expanded because they work reliably?
Which opportunities are being missed because AI capability has not been invested in enough?
Where is underinvestment limiting impact?
What must be funded before AI can support growth?
In recent losses, where did competitors expose AI weakness?
Which opportunities showed weaker AI maturity versus alternatives?
Where is AI positioning not competitive?
Where is AI creating risk to trust with users or clients?
Which use cases introduce uncertainty the business cannot manage?
Where does AI weaken confidence?
Which AI use cases cannot scale reliably?
Where does AI work in pilots but fail at scale?
Which capabilities break under broader adoption?
Which opportunities should be avoided even if winnable because AI expectations are too high to meet?
Where would winning create delivery risk due to AI gaps?
Which deals depend too heavily on unproven AI capability?
